title: Level 3: The Stolen Research Drive
briefing: A research drive containing unpublished project data disappeared from a locked storage cabinet. Several people had partial access, but the clues are conflicting.
solutionExplanation: Farhan had cabinet access through a borrowed key, was present during the unexplained cabinet opening, and his laptop later connected to a drive matching the missing device ID.

suspects.name: Dr. Kavya Menon
suspects.description: Owned the research drive and controlled the cabinet key.

suspects.name: Farhan Qureshi
suspects.description: Needed access to old experiment data for a presentation.

suspects.name: Isha Verma
suspects.description: Worked with copied datasets but did not usually access physical drives.

suspects.name: Manoj Bhat
suspects.description: Entered the lab for air-conditioning repair.

evidence.title: Cabinet Access Register
evidence.content: The storage cabinet key was signed out by Dr. Kavya at 10:00 AM and returned at 10:20 AM. Later, a duplicate key was signed out by Farhan Qureshi at 2:10 PM for checking archived files.

evidence.title: Witness Statement - Farhan Qureshi
evidence.content: Farhan says he borrowed the duplicate key but only opened the cabinet to check a printed file list. He says he did not remove any drive.

evidence.title: Cabinet Sensor Log
evidence.content: The storage cabinet was opened at 2:16 PM and closed at 2:21 PM. No other cabinet opening was recorded after that until the missing drive was reported.

evidence.title: USB Device Forensic Report
evidence.content: A laptop assigned to Farhan connected to a USB storage device at 2:34 PM. The connected device serial number ended with X9A2, matching the missing research drive inventory record.

evidence.title: Research Drive Inventory
evidence.content: The missing research drive is listed as Model SecureData V2, serial number RD-77-X9A2. It was last physically verified in the cabinet at 10:15 AM.

evidence.title: Witness Statement - Isha Verma
evidence.content: Isha says she worked remotely on a cleaned copy of the dataset and did not enter the research lab after lunch. Wi-Fi logs show her laptop connected from the library area between 2:00 PM and 3:00 PM.

evidence.title: Maintenance Work Order
evidence.content: Manoj Bhat repaired the air-conditioning vent between 1:30 PM and 1:55 PM. He was escorted by lab assistant staff and did not access the storage cabinet area.

evidence.title: Lab CCTV Note
evidence.content: CCTV near the lab entrance shows Farhan entering at 2:12 PM and leaving at 2:26 PM carrying a laptop sleeve. The camera angle does not clearly show whether a drive was inside the sleeve.

evidence.title: Incident Timeline
evidence.content: 10:15 AM: Drive verified in cabinet. 1:30 PM: Maintenance entered lab. 1:55 PM: Maintenance left. 2:10 PM: Farhan signed duplicate key. 2:16 PM: Cabinet opened. 2:26 PM: Farhan left lab. 2:34 PM: Farhan laptop connected to USB drive ending X9A2. 3:10 PM: Drive reported missing.
